title: Senator Shannon Grove (R-Bakersfield) is proud to announce the introduction of Senate Bill 64 (SB 64) and Senate Constitutional Amendment 1 (SCA 1), together these companion measur
prompt_preview:

Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The School Choice Flex Account Act of 2025 aims to transform K-12 education in California by empowering parents to select the accredited school that best suits their child’s unique needs.",
    "California spends $24,700 per student, which is well above the national average, yet consistently ranks near the bottom in academic achievement among 4th and 8th graders.",
    "Many parents believe that the current education system fails to prepare students for college and careers despite significant investment.",
    "Students of color, particularly African American and Latino students, disproportionately face challenges in meeting grade-level standards in English Language Arts and Mathematics.",
    "Many students must attend underperforming schools due to being determined by their zip code.",
    "Current policies often exclude students in special education programs from attending religious private schools with funding assistance.",
    "Senate Bill 64 proposes to establish a School Choice Flex Account providing $8,000 for tuition and education-related expenses for students.",
    "Senate Bill 64 also proposes to create a Special Education Flex Account offering $16,000 for students with special needs.",
    "Unused balances in the proposed flex accounts will revert to the state’s General Fund for K-12 education.",
    "Local and federal funding will remain allocated to public schools, increasing per-pupil spending for students who continue in their local schools.",
    "Senate Bill 64 and Senate Constitutional Amendment 1 aim to deliver a more dynamic education system that provides tailored access to learning environments for students.",
    "The legislation seeks to ensure that families of all backgrounds have equal opportunities to seek quality education, including those in special education programs."
  ]
}
```
